Kirkpatrick, George (MRN X012680314) DOB: 11/13/1946 Encounter Date: 03/31/2026
Patient currently borrowing friends oxygen cylinder
Recommend to call office if there is consistent low pulse OX
Patient verbalizes understanding of treatment plan.
George Kirkpatrick is a 79y.o. male here for Follow-up
A 79-year-old male with hypertension, prior tobacco use, and recent changes in sleep medication presents
for evaluation of low oxygen saturation and new-onset gait instability.
Since March 27, 2026, he has experienced significant unsteadiness while walking, requiring assistance
and a walker at home. This visit marks his first outing since these symptoms began. Supplemental oxygen
has been used at home, with saturation consistently around 87% by pulse oximeter, dropping further when
oxygen is removed. He does not report shortness of breath. A caregiver recommended oxygen use due to
episodes of confusion. There is no history of chronic lung disease, though he has seen a pulmonologist
and underwent evaluation during a prior hospitalization. He quit smoking 45 years ago.
Sleep disturbances developed following a reduction in Seroquel dosage from three to two, and then to one
tablet, a change made during a hospital admission due to low blood pressure. He currently takes Seroquel
300 mg at night and mirtazapine but continues to have difficulty sleeping through the night, often waking at
2 or 3 AM and sleeping on the couch. His appetite remains stable.
Blood pressure is managed with amlodipine 5 mg daily.
- Reports disrupted sleep, waking at 2-3 AM and sleeping on the couch
